title: Illipe maclayana
intro: Illipe maclayana is a tropical plant species native to the rainforests of Malaysia and Indonesia, with its seeds being a rich source of vegetable fat. The seeds of this plant have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including skin problems, wounds, and fever, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Illipe maclayana include illipe butter, which is rich in fatty acids such as oleic acid, stearic acid, and palmitic acid, as well as other compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. In traditional herbal medicine, Illipe maclayana is commonly prepared as an ointment or cream to treat skin conditions, or as a tincture or decoction to treat internal health issues.
taxonomy: Illipe maclayana is classified as a species of tree in the family Sapindaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The Sapindaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the soapberry or soapbark family. This family comprises over 1,400 species, including trees, shrubs, and climbing plants. The classification of Illipe maclayana as a member of the Sapindaceae family provides insight into its evolutionary relationships and botanical characteristics.
